title: Hát Bội Saigon Art Cruise
article:
The programme began with a practical editorial question: how could Hát Bội be introduced to a contemporary audience without reducing it to spectacle? The answer combined cultural research, performance context and a narrative structure designed for an intimate journey on the Saigon River.

My role connected curatorial framing with execution. I developed the programme story, performance script, audience information and communication materials, keeping the language accessible while respecting the form's cultural specificity.

title: Culture Writing for Tatler Vietnam
article:
My work for Tatler Vietnam focuses on culture, art, design and contemporary life. Each feature begins with research and a clear editorial angle, then develops through reporting, interviews and contextual writing.

The selected archive represents both digital and print work. Rather than treating culture as decoration, the articles connect individual practices with broader questions about memory, place and changing ways of living.

title: Brand Copywriting System
article:
At Toong, copywriting extended beyond campaign headlines. I worked across social media, internal communication, email marketing, landing pages, factsheets, brochures and launch materials.

The central task was consistency: each format needed its own pace and level of detail while still sounding like the same brand. Place, architecture and customer experience became recurring editorial materials rather than isolated selling points.

title: Hidden Champions Show
article:
The podcast writing process combined guest research, episode structure and conversational pacing. The scripts needed to clarify complex business ideas without making the exchange sound overproduced.

I developed research led questions and narrative pathways for more than ten episodes. The resulting work reached over 100,000 total views and remains available through the programme's official YouTube archive.

title: Cuộc Đua Đầu Thai
article:
This personal design exercise explores Vietnamese supernatural folklore through a compact race game. The project combines character concepts, a shared race board and a playful reincarnation narrative.

It sits outside my professional commissions, but reflects the same interest in turning research and cultural material into an accessible experience.

title: Lela Journal Editorial Archive
article: The archive brings together profiles, interviews, cultural reporting, advertorials and editorial features. The work strengthened my ability to move between research, interviewing and publication ready writing while maintaining a clear human angle.

title: NovelToon Editorial and Community
article: This role combined fiction editing with author development and community content. I managed serial narratives, developed assessment frameworks and prepared practical writing curriculum for emerging authors.

title: Books Editing and Translation
article: The selected covers represent a wider print portfolio across psychology, family, business and general nonfiction. My work covered manuscript editing, translation coordination and publication readiness.

title: Lọng Bướm
article: The exhibition connects craft, personal memory and the changing life of butterfly parasols. The editorial work included interviews, curatorial framing, landing page copy and public communication.

title: Vàng Sơn Một Thuở
article: A public programme built around lacquer, material knowledge and the experience of makers. The project connected exhibition structure with artist context and communication.

title: Người Đơm Hoa Giấy
article: A documentary exhibition about paper flower makers and the persistence of handcraft in contemporary city life. Research and interviews were shaped into a quiet, accessible exhibition narrative.

title: Khơi Gợi Mỹ Cảm
article: An atelier series creating approachable entry points into Vietnamese art and aesthetic attention. The work included programme framing, agenda development and communication materials.

title: Đờn Ca Tài Tử Culture Class
article: A participatory introduction to Southern Vietnamese chamber music, structured around listening, instruments and cultural context.

title: Improv Kịch Ứng Tác
article: An audience facing improv theatre programme supported by landing page copy, programme framing and event communication.

title: Client Stories: Kengur, Keyrus and ExoTrail
article: These business interviews translated customer experience and workplace culture into credible brand stories. The process covered research, questionnaires, interviews and narrative scripts.

title: SEO Editorial for Mybest.vn
article: Search led product guides developed from research, comparison frameworks and clear user intent across mobility, finance, beauty and mobile applications.

title: Selected Book Translations
article: Selected translated titles developed for Vietnamese readers, with attention to clarity, tone and publication readiness.

title: Film Criticism
article: A selected archive examining image, narrative, gender and cultural interpretation in cinema.

title: E Magazine and Branded Editorial
article: Digital magazine work spanning travel, gender, communication, business profiles and seasonal culture.

title: People, Ideas and City Life
article: Interviews with artists, entrepreneurs, doctors, specialists and cultural figures, shaped into clear editorial profiles.

title: Artist Talks and Cultural Interviews
article: Question frameworks and conversations designed to preserve artistic process, craft knowledge and personal histories behind cultural programmes.

title: Tranh Gói Vải Culture Class
article: A hands on cultural programme exploring image making, material attention and fabric wrapping practice.

title: Toong Thi Sách Launch
article: A place led launch narrative developed across teaser scripts, social content, internal communication, email marketing and sales materials.

title: Toong The Global City
article: Brand storytelling for a new workplace destination, spanning a TVC script, English factsheet and customer facing launch communication.

title: Place Narratives and Visual Direction
article: Campaign concepts and editorial direction developed across location launches, pop ups and cultural programmes in Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi.

title: Editorial and Communication Proposals
article: Strategic proposals translating research, communication objectives and programme structures into clear client narratives.

title: Fiction Adaptation and Writer Training
article: Character frameworks, adaptation scripts, editorial criteria and practical writing curriculum developed for serial fiction authors.
